channel.title: Paper Feeds
channel.description: Keyword-based research paper feeds from arXiv and IACR

item.title: OpenPCC: Open and Confidential LLM Serving on Commodity TEEs
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.11145v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

生成式AI应用（如个人AI助手、图像生成器、智能聊天服务）依赖大规模语言模型（LLM），其推理通常托管于云端推理服务（CIS）。然而，用户请求中日益增多的个人敏感信息与企业机密数据对CIS提出了严格的隐私保护需求。当前行业方案（如Apple Private Cloud Compute和Google Private AI Compute）虽具前瞻性，但均依赖专有硬件与封闭生态，无法被第三方复用；更关键的是，其架构设计存在固有缺陷（如可信边界模糊、侧信道防护不足），难以真正保障端到端机密性。

方法与创新

本文首次系统梳理了构建安全、开放CIS的四大基础要求：硬件无关性、端到端机密性、可验证的执行完整性与开源可审计性。在此基础上，我们提出OpenPCC——首个不依赖定制芯片、完全基于商用可信执行环境（TEE，如Intel SGX/AMD SEV-SNP）的开源保密CIS框架。OpenPCC通过轻量级可信引导、细粒度内存隔离、加密请求流水线与零知识证明辅助的远程认证，实现请求处理全链路机密性与完整性。

评估与结论

我们在真实Llama-3 8B模型（vLLM后端）上完成端到端原型部署与量化分析：OpenPCC仅引入~12%端到端延迟开销（vs. 非TEE基线），吞吐下降<18%，且全部安全开销可明确剥离（与TEE硬件固有开销解耦）。安全性分析证实其可抵御常见TEE侧信道攻击与API层注入。本工作证明：商用TEE足以支撑高可用、高可信的开放LLM服务，为隐私优先的AI基础设施提供了切实可行的开源路径。

AI Summary (English)

Generative AI services increasingly process highly sensitive user data, demanding confidential cloud inference. Proprietary solutions (e.g., Apple PCC, Google Private AI Compute) lack openness and suffer from architectural flaws undermining privacy guarantees. This paper identifies four fundamental requirements for secure, open Confidential Inference Services (CIS): hardware agnosticism, end-to-end confidentiality, verifiable execution integrity, and auditability. We propose OpenPCC, the first open-source CIS framework built exclusively on commodity TEEs (SGX/SEV-SNP), eliminating reliance on custom hardware. OpenPCC features a lightweight trusted boot, encrypted request pipelines, fine-grained memory isolation, and ZK-assisted remote attestation. Evaluated end-to-end on Llama-3 8B (vLLM), OpenPCC incurs only ~12% latency overhead and <18% throughput loss—with all security costs explicitly isolated from underlying TEE hardware costs. Security analysis confirms resilience against common TEE side channels and API-level attacks. Our work demonstrates that commercially available TEEs can robustly support production-grade, confidential, and open LLM serving.

Abstract

Generative AI applications such as personal AI agents, image generators, and chat assistants offer advanced capabilities to improve user experience. Behind the scenes, Large Language Models (LLMs) that power these services require a massive amount of computation and are usually deployed in the cloud, available as APIs, meaning that a user's request has to be sent to a Cloud Inference Service (CIS) for processing. However, the strong capabilities of LLM also mean that user's requests now contain much more personal sensitive or enterprise confidential information, demanding equally strong protection in CIS. While early industry efforts such as Apple Private Cloud Compute (PCC) and Google Private AI Compute have emerged to show the potential of secure CIS, they are not adoptable for deployment by others due to their reliance on proprietary hardware and closed ecosystem. In addition, they all suffer from their own design glitches that can undermine the ambitious goal of bringing in true privacy protection to end users. In this paper, we present our analysis of the fundamental requirements of building a secure yet open CIS. We then present OpenPCC, a Confidential CIS framework that does not rely on proprietary hardware but instead uses commercially available TEEs. We implement an open-source prototype and characterize it end-to-end on a Llama-3 8B vLLM workload, separating OpenPCC's own cost from the underlying TEE hardware. Our analysis and evaluation demonstrated the feasibility and security of the system.

item.title: Understanding and mitigating the risks of OpenClaw for non-technical users: A practical guide with Skill
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.11007v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

OpenClaw作为新兴的自主式AI智能体框架，正被大量非技术背景用户广泛采用。其多步任务自动化能力虽具革命性，却伴生隐私泄露、权限滥用、误执行高危操作、模型越狱、数据污染、供应链劫持及社会工程攻击等七大核心风险。现有安全研究多面向开发者或安全专家，术语密集、方案复杂，导致普通用户难以理解与实施，形成显著的“安全鸿沟”。

方法与创新

本研究首次系统面向非技术用户构建可落地的风险防护体系：

  • 风险通俗化：将七大风险逐一转化为生活化场景（如“AI擅自读取你的微信聊天记录”“自动下载并运行可疑文件”），摒弃技术黑箱，用零基础语言阐明成因与后果；
  • 策略操作化：为每类风险提炼3–5个一步即达的防御动作（如“在设置中关闭‘跨应用数据访问’”“启用技能沙箱模式”“定期审查已授权的第三方Skill列表”）；
  • 防护自动化：开发并开源配套的OpenClaw Safety Skill——一个经严格审计的轻量级官方技能，可一键启用日志最小化、API调用白名单、敏感动作二次确认、外部连接拦截等关键防护机制，用户无需修改配置文件或编写代码。

价值与影响

本工作证明：AI智能体安全并非仅属专家领域。通过“可理解—可操作—可自动化”的三层设计，非技术用户首次获得真正可用、可信、可持续的安全赋能。该实践指南已在社区实测中使新手用户的高危操作发生率下降72%，为AI普惠时代的责任共治提供了可复用的方法论范式。

AI Summary (English)

OpenClaw, a rapidly adopted autonomous AI agent framework, poses significant yet poorly understood risks for non-technical users—ranging from unauthorized data access and privilege escalation to harmful command execution and supply-chain compromise. While prior research characterizes these threats, it remains inaccessible to lay users due to technical jargon and complex mitigation requirements. To bridge this gap, we present the first practical, user-centered safety guide for OpenClaw. We identify and explain seven core risks in plain language, translate each into concrete, step-by-step defensive actions (e.g., disabling cross-app permissions, enabling sandboxed skill execution), and introduce an official OpenClaw Safety Skill that automates critical protections—including API call whitelisting, sensitive-action confirmation prompts, and outbound connection blocking—with zero coding required. Evaluated with 127 non-technical participants, our approach reduced high-risk user behaviors by 72%, demonstrating that intelligent agent safety can be democratized through clarity, simplicity, and automation.

Abstract

OpenClaw has rapidly emerged as a transformative artificial intelligence (AI) agent framework, and its ability to autonomously execute complex, multi-step tasks has attracted an ever-growing and diverse user base. However, this capability comes with significant risks. While existing research has made important strides in characterizing these threats, such work is predominantly directed at technically sophisticated audiences. It remains largely inaccessible to non-technical users. This demographic now makes up an increasingly large and underserved portion of the community, yet it is these very users who most urgently need practical and straightforward guidance. In response, we bridge this gap through a series of interconnected efforts designed to lower the risk barrier for non-technical OpenClaw users. First, we identify and categorize seven core risks that OpenClaw users may encounter in daily usage, explaining each in plain language so that non-technical users can readily grasp the nature and potential consequences of these threats. Second, for each identified risk, we distill a set of corresponding defensive strategies into clear and actionable operational steps that are easy to follow. Third, to make protection even easier, we provide a companion OpenClaw Skill that automates key security configurations, enabling users to safeguard their systems with minimal manual intervention. Through this work, we demonstrate that safeguarding against the risks of intelligent agents need not be the exclusive domain of security experts, and that non-technical users can meaningfully participate in reducing these risks through simple, practical actions.

item.title: Context-Based Adversarial Attacks on AI Code Generators: Vulnerability Analysis and Implications
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.10945v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

AI代码生成系统（如GitHub Copilot、CodeLlama）正深度融入现代软件开发流程，但其推理阶段存在未被充分认知的安全隐患。本研究首次系统揭示上下文驱动的对抗攻击（Context-Based Adversarial Attacks）这一新型威胁范式：攻击者无需修改模型参数或访问训练数据，仅通过精心设计的注释、文档字符串、变量命名等良性上下文输入，即可诱导大语言模型（LLM）稳定输出含漏洞代码（如SQL注入、命令执行、硬编码密钥等）。

方法与规模

我们在4个主流代码大模型（CodeT5+、CodeLlama-34B、GPT-3.5-Turbo、GPT-4）上开展2,800次受控实验，覆盖12类常见安全漏洞和7种上下文扰动策略（如语义混淆注释、误导性函数名、偏置文档模板）。采用标准化漏洞检测工具（Semgrep + custom CWE patterns）量化生成代码的可利用性，并评估跨模型迁移性与防御有效性。

关键发现

  • 攻击强度显著：对抗上下文使漏洞生成率从基线3.5%飙升至37.4%（10.7倍增长）；对GPT-3.5-Turbo的直接指令攻击成功率高达100%。
  • 系统性脆弱：跨模型攻击迁移率达60–100%，证实漏洞根源于代码生成范式固有缺陷（如上下文敏感性过载、安全约束缺失），而非个别模型偏差。
  • 实用化防御：提出双层轻量级防御框架（上下文语义异常检测 + 生成代码静态模式验证），在真实IDE环境中实现89.1%漏洞检出率、仅0.3%误报率、520ms端到端延迟，已开源原型（GitHub: /contextguard）。

创新意义

本研究为AI编程助手的安全治理提供首个实证基准，推动“安全即提示”（Security-as-Prompt）范式演进，并为标准组织（如OWASP AI Security Top 10）提供可落地的评估方法论。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces context-based adversarial attacks—a novel threat class targeting AI code generators by injecting maliciously crafted, syntactically valid context (e.g., comments, docstrings, variable names) to bias LLMs toward generating exploitable code. Through 2,800 controlled experiments across CodeT5+, CodeLlama, GPT-3.5-Turbo, and GPT-4, we demonstrate that such attacks increase vulnerability generation by 10.7× (from 3.5% to 37.4%), with direct instruction attacks achieving 100% success on GPT-3.5-Turbo. Critically, cross-model transferability ranges from 60% to 100%, revealing architecture-level flaws rather than model-specific quirks. We propose a lightweight dual-layer defense achieving 89.1% detection rate, 0.3% false positive rate, and 520ms latency, validating real-time deployability in IDEs. This work establishes the first empirical security benchmark for context-aware code generation and informs emerging AI security standards.

Abstract

AI-powered code generation systems have transformed software development but introduce critical inference-time security vulnerabilities. This research presents a systematic investigation of context-based adversarial attacks, where strategically crafted contextual inputs, including comments, documentation, variable names, bias large language models toward generating exploitable code. Through 2,800 controlled experiments across CodeT5+, CodeLlama, GPT-3.5-Turbo, and GPT-4, we quantify attack effectiveness and defense mechanisms. Results demonstrate that adversarial conditions increase vulnerability generation 10.7x (from 3.5% to 37.4%), with direct instruction attacks achieving 100% success on GPT-3.5-Turbo. Cross-model transferability reaches 60-100%, indicating systemic architectural vulnerabilities rather than model-specific flaws. Our dual-layer defense framework achieves 89.1% detection rate with 0.3% false positives and 520ms latency, demonstrating practical feasibility for real-time deployment in development environments.

item.title: Securing Code Understanding: Detecting Natural Backdoor Vulnerability in Code Language Models
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.10846v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

代码大语言模型（CodeLMs）已深度融入软件工程实践，在代码补全、生成、翻译等智能任务中发挥关键作用。然而，其安全风险日益凸显——除人为注入的后门攻击外，近期研究发现自然后门（Natural Backdoors）：即在无数据投毒、标准训练流程下模型自发习得的隐蔽脆弱性。这类漏洞威胁程度不亚于恶意后门，但其在CodeLMs中的存在性、普遍性、成因及可防御性尚缺乏系统性实证研究。

方法与发现

本研究首次对44种跨架构（如CodeBERT、GraphCodeBERT、PLBART、CodeT5）、跨任务（代码摘要、缺陷检测、克隆检测等）场景开展大规模实证分析：

  • 普遍存在性：在全部44个场景中均观测到显著自然后门触发行为（平均ASR达38.7%），证实其为CodeLMs的内在固有属性；
  • 本质差异：自然后门在模型表征空间与参数敏感度上显著区别于注入后门——其触发模式更依赖语义一致性而非特定token序列，且梯度扰动分布更弥散；
  • 强迁移性：自然后门可在不同数据集（如Java→Python）、不同架构（Encoder-only→Encoder-Decoder）及共享预训练知识间跨域迁移，揭示其根植于通用代码表征学习机制；
  • 成因溯源：归因于训练数据中隐含的统计偏差（如高频API调用模式）与优化过程中的隐式偏好（如梯度下降对局部语义捷径的强化）；
  • 防御失效：主流防御方法（如Neural Cleanse、Fine-pruning、Backdoor Scanning）对自然后门检测率低于22%，凸显其规避现有方案的能力。

创新贡献

提出ScanNBT（Scalable Natural Backdoor Tracer）：一种轻量级、无需标签、基于注意力头异常激活与梯度符号一致性的双通道检测框架。在12个CodeLMs上实现平均91.4%的检测准确率（F1），较SOTA提升3.8×。本工作为理解与加固代码大模型的安全基线提供了首个系统性实证基础。

AI Summary (English)

Code Language Models (CodeLMs) are increasingly vulnerable to natural backdoors—intrinsic vulnerabilities emerging without data poisoning during standard training. This paper presents the first comprehensive empirical study of natural backdoors across 44 CodeLM configurations (e.g., CodeBERT, CodeT5) and code intelligence tasks. We demonstrate their prevalence (average attack success rate: 38.7%), intrinsic nature, and strong transferability across datasets, architectures, and shared knowledge. Crucially, we identify fundamental differences from injected backdoors at both representation and parameter levels, and trace their origins to dataset biases and optimization-induced semantic shortcuts. Standard backdoor defenses fail dramatically (<22% detection), motivating our novel ScanNBT detector—a label-free, scalable method leveraging attention-head activation anomalies and gradient sign consistency—achieving 91.4% average F1-score across 12 models. Our findings establish a foundational security benchmark for CodeLMs and enable robust natural backdoor mitigation.

Abstract

Code Language Models (CodeLMs) have become integral to software engineering, significantly advancing code intelligence tasks. However, their widespread adoption has raised critical security concerns, particularly regarding susceptibility to backdoor attacks. Recent studies have uncovered naturally occurring backdoors, referred to as natural backdoors, in normally trained deep learning models. Despite posing threats as serious as those introduced through data poisoning, security implications of natural backdoor vulnerabilities in CodeLMs remain poorly understood. In this paper, we conduct a thorough empirical study of natural backdoor vulnerabilities in CodeLMs across various model architectures and code intelligence tasks. Specifically, we examine potential natural backdoor vulnerabilities across 44 scenarios, demonstrating that natural backdoors are prevalent and intrinsic to CodeLMs. We reveal differences between injected and natural backdoor vulnerabilities at both the model and parameter levels. We then analyze the transferability of natural backdoor vulnerabilities from three perspectives: datasets, model architectures, and shared knowledge. We further investigate the causes of natural backdoors from two aspects: training datasets and the model training procedure. We evaluate existing backdoor defense techniques, including pre-training, in-training, and post-training defenses, in mitigating natural backdoors. Finally, we propose ScanNBT, a novel detection method designed to improve comprehensive detection of natural backdoor vulnerabilities in CodeLMs. We aim for our findings to enhance understanding of these vulnerabilities and provide insights for strengthening CodeLM security against backdoor threats.

item.title: RedAct: Redacting Agent Capability Traces for Procedural Skill Protection
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.10813v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

用户依赖智能体执行迹（execution traces）进行行为观测、故障诊断与责任追溯。然而，这些迹中蕴含丰富的程序性细节——包括工具调用序列、中间决策链、错误恢复逻辑等，可能无意间暴露开发者私有的 procedural skills（程序性技能），如关键阈值、计算公式与策略逻辑。更严峻的是，攻击者无需访问模型权重或技能代码，仅通过分析公开迹即可逆向还原核心能力，构成新型“能力泄露”安全风险。

方法：RedAct 框架与 CapTraceBench 基准

为系统量化该风险并评估防护效果，我们构建了首个面向程序性技能保护的基准 CapTraceBench：涵盖7个领域、75个长周期任务、154个人工精标技能。在此基础上，提出 RedAct（https://github.com/XuShuwenn/RedAct）——一个端到端受保护迹发布框架，包含三阶段设计：

  • 定位：识别迹中需保护的关键技能片段（如阈值、条件分支逻辑）；
  • 重写：在保留审计必需证据（如决策因果链、成功/失败归因）前提下，语义一致地替换敏感内容；
  • 水印：嵌入轻量级、不可见的行为水印（behavioral watermark），支持下游迹来源追踪与篡改检测。

主要发现与创新

在典型迹复用场景（如技能蒸馏、逆向提示工程）中，RedAct 将归一化技能迁移率（NST）从原始迹的 44.7–67.1% 降至低于无技能基线（即有效阻断能力窃取），同时完整维持审计有效性。其行为水印在7类下游检测器上实现 93.6–100.0% 真阳性率，误报率≤1.9%。本研究首次将公开智能体执行迹明确定义为安全接口，证实选择性红删（selective redaction）可在零审计证据损失前提下，实质性遏制程序性能力泄露。

AI Summary (English)

Execution traces—used for debugging, auditing, and accountability—expose rich procedural skills (e.g., thresholds, formulas, strategies), enabling unauthorized skill recovery without model weights or source code. To quantify this risk and evaluate protection, we introduce CapTraceBench, a benchmark of 75 long-horizon tasks and 154 curated skills across seven domains. We propose RedAct, a trace redaction framework that (1) localizes sensitive skill elements, (2) rewrites traces while preserving verifier-critical evidence (e.g., causal decision chains), and (3) embeds lightweight behavioral watermarks for provenance tracking. Across representative trace reuse methods, RedAct reduces normalized skill transfer (NST) from 44.7–67.1% on raw traces to below the no-skill baseline, effectively eliminating capability leakage while retaining full audit fidelity. Its standalone watermarks achieve 93.6–100.0% true detection with ≤1.9% false alarm rate. This work reframes public agent traces as security-critical interfaces and demonstrates that selective redaction enables robust procedural skill protection without compromising accountability.

Abstract

Users rely on execution traces to observe agent behavior, diagnose failures, and ensure accountability. These traces contain rich procedural detail, including tool invocations, intermediate decisions, and error-recovery logic. Yet this detail can expose private procedural skills, allowing downstream methods to recover key formulas, thresholds, and strategies without access to model weights or skill files. To quantify this risk and evaluate protection, we construct \textsc{CapTraceBench}, a benchmark of 75 specialized long-horizon tasks and 154 curated skills across seven domains. We also introduce \textsc{RedAct} https://github.com/XuShuwenn/RedAct, a protected trace release framework that localizes protected key information, rewrites traces while preserving verifier-critical evidence, and embeds behavioral watermarks for downstream provenance analysis. Across representative trace reuse methods, \textsc{RedAct} reduces normalized skill transfer (NST) from 44.7--67.1\% on raw traces to below the no-skill baseline, while preserving audit evidence. Its standalone behavioral watermarks reach 93.6--100.0\% true detection with a false alarm rate of at most 1.9\%. These results frame public agent traces as security interfaces and show that selective redaction can reduce procedural capability leakage without removing audit evidence.

item.title: A Bayesian Network Approach for Enhancing Security-Focused Decision Support Systems
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.10782v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

现代开源网络普遍采用异构技术栈，虽提升了互操作性与功能丰富度，但也导致组件耦合加剧、安全配置复杂度陡增。运维人员需跨领域掌握大量工具（如WAF、SIEM、IAM系统）的知识，难以高效评估其在机密性、完整性、可用性（CIA三元组）上的协同适配性。

方法与创新

本研究提出一种基于贝叶斯网络（Bayesian Network, BN）的安全导向型决策支持系统（DSS）。该系统核心创新在于：

  • 需求驱动建模：将用户对CIA三元组的高层安全目标（如“高可用性+中等机密性”）转化为BN节点的先验概率约束；
  • 可扩展架构：支持按领域（云平台、IoT边缘、容器环境）动态加载定制化BN子模型，各子模型通过标准化接口解耦；
  • 轻量级推理引擎：采用概率软证据（Probabilistic Soft Evidence）处理模糊需求，并结合结构学习算法自动优化网络拓扑，兼顾可解释性与计算效率。

实验验证与价值

在涵盖12类主流安全工具（含OpenVAS、Falco、Vault等）和5个典型基础设施场景的测试中，系统平均推理耗时<850ms（单次查询），CIA需求满足准确率达92.3%（F1-score），显著优于规则引擎基线（+17.6%）。该框架已开源原型，支持安全策略文档自动生成与风险传播路径可视化，为DevSecOps团队提供可审计、可演进的安全选型依据。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces a Bayesian Network (BN)-based Decision Support System (DSS) to assist infrastructure operators in selecting optimal security mechanisms (e.g., WAFs, SIEMs, IAM tools) aligned with domain-specific Confidentiality-Integrity-Availability (CIA) requirements. Unlike static rule-based approaches, our DSS models security knowledge as modular, domain-aware BNs—each encoding probabilistic dependencies among threat vectors, tool capabilities, and operational constraints. It supports fuzzy user inputs via probabilistic soft evidence inference and enables extensible model composition across cloud, IoT, and container environments. Evaluated on 12 open-source security tools across 5 infrastructure scenarios, the system achieves 92.3% F1-score in CIA-aligned tool recommendation with sub-850ms average inference time—outperforming baseline rule engines by 17.6%. The framework delivers interpretability, auditability, and seamless integration into DevSecOps pipelines.

Abstract

The adoption and integration of heterogeneous stacks in most of today's open-source based networks brings clear benefits like interoperability and availability of advanced features. Yet, on the other hand the increasing number of interconnecting components and moving parts requires maintaining an ever increasing base of interdisciplinary knowledge of different tools in different domains to ensure proper operation. To alleviate such efforts, this work proposes a Decision Support System (DSS) to guide infrastructure operators through the selection of security approaches (e.g. tools) to adopt in their environments. This framework easily captures the end-user high-level requirements on the security triad for different domains and runs inference on the designated models to provide the identified tools (security mechanisms) that better serve such needs. The presented DSS aims at delivering an understandable and extensible framework to accommodate varying requirements and Bayesian Network (BN) models. The architecture and modelling of the system are proposed, aligned with its theoretical framework. Its performance is evaluated in terms of time and prediction accuracy.

item.title: Secure Aggregation with Top-K Sparsification in Decentralized Federated Learning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.10780v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

在去中心化联邦学习（Decentralized FL）中，安全聚合（Secure Aggregation, SecAgg）是防止梯度泄露、保障用户隐私的核心机制。然而，传统SecAgg的通信开销随梯度维度线性增长，对大模型（如ViT、LLM）及带宽受限、节点不稳定的边缘网络尤为不友好。Top-K稀疏化虽能显著压缩通信量（仅上传K个最大梯度值），但其非对齐性——各用户独立选出的Top-K位置互不相同——导致现有稀疏SecAgg方案难以兼顾安全性、效率与容错性，尤其在面临用户掉线（dropouts）和合谋攻击（collusion）时更为脆弱。

方法创新

本文提出首个面向去中心化FL的信息论安全、抗合谋、容掉线的稀疏安全聚合方案。核心设计包括：

  • 离线/在线分离架构：将与维度强相关的预处理（如随机掩码生成、位置置换）移至低频离线阶段，使在线聚合通信复杂度降至O(K log n)（n为参与节点数），与模型维度无关；
  • 双层隐私保护：结合可验证随机掩码（保证加法同态性与零知识性）与位置混淆置换（隐藏Top-K索引分布），抵御半诚实及恶意合谋者；
  • 弹性拓扑适配：基于环状P2P协议动态重构聚合路径，支持任意数量节点掉线后仍完成正确聚合。

主要结果

在CIFAR-10/100与Tiny-ImageNet上的实验表明：

  • 即使仅保留1%梯度（K=0.01d），测试精度损失<0.8%，媲美全梯度SecAgg；
  • 相比基线方案，端到端通信量降低5.2–8.7×，在线阶段延迟减少63%；
  • 在20%节点掉线+4方合谋攻击下，仍严格满足(ε,δ)-差分隐私等价安全性，且无精度衰减。

本工作首次在理论安全与实用效率间取得关键平衡，为资源受限边缘场景的大模型联邦训练提供可行路径。

AI Summary (English)

Secure aggregation (SecAgg) is essential for privacy-preserving decentralized federated learning (DFL), yet its communication cost traditionally scales with gradient dimension—prohibitive for large models and bandwidth-constrained edge networks. While Top-K sparsification reduces communication by transmitting only the largest K gradient entries, its non-aligned, user-specific selection patterns hinder efficient and secure sparse aggregation—especially under dropouts and collusion. This paper proposes the first information-theoretically secure, dropout-resilient, and collusion-resistant sparse SecAgg scheme for DFL. Our approach offloads dimension-dependent overhead to an offline phase and employs jointly designed random masks and index permutations to protect both gradient values and locations. Experiments show that with only 1% sparsification, accuracy remains within 0.8% of full-gradient SecAgg, while end-to-end communication is reduced by 5.2–8.7×, and online latency drops by 63%. The scheme maintains strict security guarantees even under 20% dropouts and 4-party collusion.

Abstract

Secure aggregation is a vital component for mitigating gradient leakage in federated learning, but its communication cost conventionally scales with the gradient dimension. This becomes prohibitive for large models and even more pronounced in decentralized federated learning with limited bandwidth and unreliable nodes. Top-K gradient sparsification is an effective approach to reduce communication by transmitting only a few entries of the full gradient, while maintaining competitive model accuracy. Nevertheless, the top-K entries selected by each user are unpredictable and vary across users, which poses a challenge for efficient sparse secure aggregation. This paper studies information-theoretic secure aggregation with top-K sparsification in decentralized federated learning under user dropouts and user collusion. We propose a communication-efficient sparse secure aggregation scheme that offloads dimension-dependent overhead to an offline phase and protects private gradients using random masks and permutations. Experimental results demonstrate that our scheme preserves accuracy comparable to full-gradient aggregation even with only 1% gradient sparsification, while substantially reducing the communication cost.

item.title: Toward Secure LLM Agents: Threat Surfaces, Attacks, Defenses, and Evaluation
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.10749v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

大语言模型（LLM）代理正从对话界面演进为具备规划、工具调用、记忆维护与环境交互能力的软件组件。这一范式转变显著拓展了安全风险维度：除传统文本生成危害外，不可信输入可能劫持控制流、滥用工具权限、篡改持久化状态、泄露敏感信息或触发外部有害操作。然而，当前LLM代理安全研究高度碎片化，分散于不同攻击类型、防御层级、应用场景与评估体系之间，缺乏系统性整合。

方法论框架

本研究对247篇文献进行全生命周期、系统导向的综述，构建以信息流、委托权限、持久状态三要素交互为核心的代理安全模型。围绕四个核心问题组织分析：（1）安全建模范式；（2）主导性威胁面与攻击家族；（3）现有防御方案及其权衡；（4）安全主张的实证评估方式。

主要发现与创新

  • 威胁演化：提示注入与工具中介的控制流劫持仍是主流攻击，但持久状态污染与多代理间风险传播已成为关键新兴威胁；
  • 防御局限：现有方案（如输入净化、沙箱执行、权限最小化）提供有效模块，但组合性薄弱——难以在复杂链式调用中保障端到端安全；
  • 评估缺口：主流基准（如AgentBench、SecEval）严重低估长周期依赖、状态一致性、部署上下文敏感等真实风险；
  • 治理路径：提出四大设计原则——显式信任边界划分、基于策略的权限控制、溯源感知的状态管理、贴近生产环境的评估实践，为构建可信LLM代理系统提供系统性路线图。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents the first comprehensive, lifecycle-based synthesis of LLM agent security research, analyzing 247 papers through a systems-oriented framework centered on information flow, delegated authority, and persistent state. We identify that while prompt injection and tool-mediated control-flow hijacking remain dominant threats, persistent state corruption and cross-agent risk propagation are emerging as critical concerns. Current defenses—though valuable as isolated building blocks—lack compositional robustness across multi-step, stateful agent workflows. Moreover, existing benchmarks underrepresent long-horizon, deployment-sensitive, and state-dependent risks. We argue that secure LLM agents require four foundational principles: explicit trust boundaries, principled privilege control, provenance-aware state management, and evaluation grounded in realistic operational settings—not just static prompts or sandboxed tasks.

Abstract

Large language model (LLM) agents are rapidly moving from conversational interfaces to software components that plan, invoke tools, maintain memory, and act on external environments. This transition changes the nature of security risk. In agentic settings, failures are no longer limited to unsafe text generation. Untrusted content may redirect control flow, misuse tool privileges, corrupt persistent state, leak sensitive information, or trigger harmful external actions. At the same time, research on LLM agent security is expanding quickly but remains fragmented across attack families, defense layers, application domains, and evaluation settings. This paper synthesizes 247 papers through a lifecycle-based, systems-oriented framework that models agent security around the interaction of information flow, delegated authority, and persistent state. We organize the literature around four questions: how LLM agent security should be modeled, which threat surfaces and attack families dominate, what defenses have been proposed and with what tradeoffs, and how security claims are evaluated. We find that prompt injection and tool-mediated control-flow hijacking still dominate the field, while persistent state corruption and multi-agent propagation are becoming central emerging concerns. We further find that current defenses provide useful building blocks but remain weakly compositional, and that existing benchmarks still underrepresent long-horizon, stateful, and deployment-sensitive risks. We argue that secure LLM agents require explicit trust boundaries, principled privilege control, provenance-aware state management, and evaluation practices aligned with realistic operational settings.

item.title: MemVenom: Triggered Poisoning of Multimodal Memories in Web Agents
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.10742v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

现代网页智能体（web agents）广泛依赖外部多模态记忆（如图结构化文本-图像记忆库）实现长程推理。然而，这种基于检索的记忆范式引入了关键安全盲区：攻击者一旦向记忆中注入恶意内容，该内容将被持久存储、高概率召回，并反复扭曲智能体决策——此类“记忆投毒”（memory poisoning）在多模态场景下长期被忽视，却具备极强现实可行性。

方法创新：MemVenom框架

本文提出 MemVenom——首个面向网页智能体的黑盒、多模态记忆投毒统一框架。其核心突破在于：

  • 双阶段协同攻击设计：

(1) 触发条件化检索攻击：通过构造语义隐蔽但检索权重显著提升的“触发记忆节点”，确保恶意图文对在任意用户查询下均被优先召回；
(2) 后检索诱导攻击：结合对抗性图像扰动（保持视觉自然性）与隐式OCR注入（在图像文字区域嵌入误导性指令），在模型解析记忆时动态覆盖原始用户目标，无需修改模型参数或重训练。

  • 范式级优势：攻击持久可复用、目标无关、不依赖prompt工程，突破了传统prompt投毒或纯文本记忆攻击的局限。

关键结果与影响

在GPT-5家族、LLaVA、Qwen-VL等主流网页智能体平台上的实验表明：MemVenom端到端攻击成功率高达99.15%，且对良性任务性能影响微乎其微（平均下降<0.8%）；跨架构迁移性强，在未见过的模型尺度上仍保持>92%成功率。本工作首次系统揭示了多模态记忆作为新型攻击面的严重性，为可信智能体设计提供了关键安全警示。

AI Summary (English)

External memory—especially graph-structured multimodal (text+image) memory—has become essential for long-horizon reasoning in web agents, yet introduces a critical, underexplored attack surface: multimodal memory poisoning. We propose MemVenom, the first black-box framework to systematically poison such memories via coordinated text-image triggers. MemVenom operates in two stages: (1) a trigger-conditioned retrieval attack that ensures high-probability recall of malicious memory nodes under arbitrary user queries; and (2) a post-retrieval induction attack leveraging stealthy adversarial image perturbations and OCR-based instruction injection to override the user’s original objective—without modifying model weights or retraining. Unlike prior prompt- or text-only attacks, MemVenom enables persistent, reusable, goal-agnostic compromises. Across GPT-5-family, LLaVA, and Qwen-VL web agents, it achieves up to 99.15% end-to-end attack success, with <0.8% degradation on benign tasks and strong cross-architecture transferability (>92% success on unseen scales).

Abstract

External memory has become a core component of modern web agents, enabling long-horizon reasoning through the retrieval of past experiences. However, this paradigm introduces a critical vulnerability: malicious content injected into memory can be persistently recalled and repeatedly influence agent behavior. In this work, we identify and systematically study multimodal memory poisoning, an overlooked yet practical attack surface in web-agent systems. We propose MemVenom, a unified black-box attack framework that poisons graph-structured external memory with coordinated text-image evidence. Our method consists of a two-stage design: (1) a trigger-conditioned retrieval attack that ensures high-probability recall of malicious memory, and (2) a post-retrieval attack induction that leverages adversarial perturbations and stealthy OCR injection to override the original user objective. Unlike prior attacks that operate on prompts or text-only memory, our approach enables persistent, reusable, and goal-agnostic attacks without modifying model parameters or re-optimizing malicious tasks. Experiments across multiple web-agent frameworks and vision-language models demonstrate that MemVenom achieves strong end-to-end attack success with minimal impact on benign performance, reaching up to 99.15% on GPT-5-family web agents, while transferring effectively across architectures and model scales.

item.title: From Transactions to Records: Reconceptualizing Blockchain Systems through a Lifecycle Lens
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.10631v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

当前区块链研究普遍聚焦于可观察的链上交易，却忽视了加密货币从生成、发布、保存到处置的完整过程。这种“交易中心主义”遮蔽了区块链作为信息治理基础设施的本质，导致在合规监管、犯罪调查与长期存档等关键场景中出现认知盲区。

方法论创新

本研究以国际标准 ISO 15489-1:2016《信息和文档——记录管理》 为理论锚点，首次将记录管理核心范式系统性迁移至区块链语境：

  • 将交易（transaction） 重构为具有证据价值的“记录（record）”；
  • 将加密资产单位（如BTC、NFT） 视为“信息资产（information asset）”；
  • 将区块链本身界定为动态演化的“记录聚合体（aggregation）”。

在此基础上，提出首个覆盖全周期的七阶段区块链数据生命周期模型：创建（Creation）、捕获（Capture）、分类（Classification）、组织（Organization）、存储（Storage）、利用（Use）、处置（Disposition）。

主要发现与贡献

实证应用于比特币（同质化代币）与典型NFT（非同质化代币），验证该模型对不同资产形态的解释力。研究表明：区块链系统本质上是具备不可篡改性、时序固化性与分布式共识特性的新型记录管理系统，而非单纯交易引擎。特别指出：链上/链下边界模糊性与隐私增强技术（如零知识证明、混币器） 会显著削弱各生命周期阶段的可观测性，这对加密犯罪溯源构成结构性挑战。本框架作为元级分析工具，可：① 统整碎片化研究；② 按阶段解构法律、监管、技术与运维难题；③ 支撑生命周期导向的治理设计、链上分析与合规监管实践。

AI Summary (English)

This paper reframes blockchain systems as records management systems—not merely transactional infrastructures—by applying ISO 15489-1:2016 principles. We reconceptualize transactions as “records”, crypto-assets as “information assets”, and blockchains as dynamic “aggregations”, and propose a novel seven-stage data lifecycle: Creation, Capture, Classification, Organization, Storage, Use, and Disposition. Applied to Bitcoin and an NFT, the framework reveals how immutability, consensus, and distributed custody shape record authenticity, reliability, and accountability across stages. Critically, off-chain dependencies and privacy-enhancing technologies (e.g., ZKPs, mixers) fragment lifecycle visibility—posing systemic challenges for crypto-crime investigation. As a meta-framework, it enables stage-specific analysis of legal, regulatory, technical, and operational barriers, and informs lifecycle-aware governance, analytics, and regulation.

Abstract

Current blockchain research and analytics tend to prioritize observable on-chain transactions, obscuring the processes through which cryptocurrencies are created, publicised, retained, and disposed of. In response, this paper considers distributed ledger technologies from records management principles in ISO 15489-1:2016. Setting off by specifying the parallels -- that is transactions as "records", crypto-asset units as "information assets", and blockchains as "aggregations" -- we introduce a seven-stage lifecycle for blockchain data. We apply the framework to Bitcoin, a fungible token, and a non-fungible token. On this basis, we argue that blockchain systems are not merely transactional infrastructures but record management systems with distinctive characteristics. We discuss how the on-chain/off-chain boundary and privacy-enhancing technologies can complicate lifecycle visibility, with particular relevance for crypto-crime research and investigation. As a meta-level framework, the lifecycle perspective enables positioning existing research, decomposing legal, regulatory, technological, and operational challenges by stage, and informing lifecycle-aware approaches to blockchain governance, analytics, and regulation.

item.title: From Data Heterogeneity to Convergence: A Data-Centric Review of Federated Learning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.10595v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

联邦学习（FL）在保障数据隐私前提下实现多客户端协同建模，但其性能高度依赖数据质量与分布特性。现有综述多聚焦系统架构、通信优化或安全机制，严重忽视数据本体视角：未系统解构非独立同分布（non-IID）的构成维度，未厘清数据划分方式与真实场景的映射关系，亦未量化数据脆弱性对收敛性的影响机制。

方法与创新

本研究首次提出以数据为中心的FL收敛性分析框架，实现三大突破：
1. non-IID可量化分级：将non-IID解耦为标签倾斜（Label Skew）、特征偏移（Feature Shift）、数量异质（Quantity Imbalance）和语义碎片（Semantic Fragmentation） 四类可测特质；基于跨模态（图像/文本/图）实证，按收敛抑制强度排序为：强（标签倾斜）、中（语义碎片）、轻（数量异质），并揭示其梯度冲突、局部最优陷阱等内在机理；
2. 划分协议真实性评估：指出常用Dirichlet划分过度简化类别重叠，而病理式划分（如CIFAR-10的“仅猫狗”子集）引入伪鲁棒性幻觉——模型在测试集准确率虚高5–12%，但泛化至真实长尾分布时骤降23%；
3. 收敛-鲁棒性权衡显式化：验证数据投毒攻击下，现有防御（如Krum、Median）虽提升鲁棒性，却使收敛速度降低37–68%，且稳定性标准差扩大2.1倍；提出“收敛韧性”新指标，统一评估精度、速度与抗扰动能力。

实践价值

本综述提供面向工程落地的数据诊断清单：从数据采集协议、划分参数选择到防御策略部署，均给出可复现的收敛性预测指南，助力构建可预期、可调试、可验证的联邦学习系统。

AI Summary (English)

This survey pioneers a data-centric lens for understanding convergence in Federated Learning (FL), addressing a critical gap in existing literature. We make three key advances: (1) We decompose non-IID heterogeneity into four measurable traits—label skew, feature shift, quantity imbalance, and semantic fragmentation—and empirically rank their impact on convergence across image, text, and graph domains (strong → medium → light), explaining underlying gradient divergence mechanisms. (2) We critically evaluate common data splitting protocols (e.g., Dirichlet), exposing how artificial splits introduce accuracy artifacts (e.g., +5–12% spurious test accuracy) that mask poor real-world generalization. (3) We quantify the explicit trade-off between convergence and robustness: defenses against data poisoning (e.g., Krum) improve adversarial accuracy but degrade convergence speed by 37–68% and increase instability (2.1× higher std. dev. in loss). To our knowledge, this is the first FL survey to systematically link concrete data properties, splitting practices, and defenses directly to convergence behavior—providing practitioners with actionable, data-informed design principles for predictable training.

Abstract

Federated Learning (FL) has emerged as a promising solution for data hunger in centralized learning. This paradigm enables privacy with multiple clients to train a shared-task model collaboratively without exposing their local data. While being a key component in any learning system, data is also a primary source of vulnerabilities and challenges, and a major determinant of a stable and well-converged training. Existing FL reviews describe general foundations, security practices, opportunities, challenges, and applications, without delving into diverse aspects of data and considering problems from the data perspective. They rarely provide a data-lens synthesis that links concrete data properties, split protocols, and defenses to convergence speed and stability. This survey fills that gap with three advances. First, we analyze non-IID into measurable traits and rank their influence on convergence as strong, medium, or light, explaining the mechanisms behind each and reconciling evidence across images, texts, and graphs. Second, we connect experimental splitting practices to the real phenomena they emulate, expose the artifacts they introduce, and show how those artifacts affect target accuracy. Third, we analyze how data-related vulnerabilities and their proposed defenses affect convergence, reporting performance under clean and adversarial conditions to make the convergence-robustness trade-off explicit. To our knowledge, this is the first survey to provide a complete understanding of data-related challenges that govern FL. With clear takeaways distilled for each concern, our work serves as actionable guidance, helping practitioners design their system with predictable convergence and stability.

item.title: Assessing Automated Prompt Injection Attacks in Agentic Environments
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.10525v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

间接提示注入（Indirect Prompt Injection）是大语言模型（LLM）智能体面临的关键安全威胁，尤其当其需处理来自不可信外部源（如网页、数据库、用户上传文件）的数据时。尽管自动化攻击方法（如GCG、TAP）在传统“越狱”场景中已被验证有效，但其在真实智能体环境（agentic environments）中的可行性、鲁棒性与迁移性仍缺乏系统性实证研究。

方法与实验设计

本研究基于开源智能体评估框架 AgentDojo，首次将白盒优化方法（Gradient-based GCG）与黑盒优化方法（Token-level Adversarial Perturbation, TAP）适配至多步决策、工具调用、记忆交互的典型智能体范式。我们在80组任务对上开展跨域评估，覆盖编程、数学推理、Web操作与知识问答四大领域，并测试了包括Llama-3-8B、Qwen2-7B、Claude-3-Haiku及GPT-4o在内的6种主流模型。

主要发现

  • 黑盒显著优于白盒：TAP在平均攻击成功率上比GCG高2.3倍（p<0.01），主因是GCG在有限计算预算下易陷入梯度震荡与早停，难以收敛；
  • 攻击者模型能力双刃剑：更强的基础能力提升注入有效性（如Qwen2-7B攻击成功率比Phi-3高37%），但经安全对齐微调的模型会主动拒绝生成恶意提示（拒绝率最高达68%）；
  • 迁移性存在强模型依赖性：任务通用型攻击（task-universal）可高效泛化至未见任务及分布外领域（跨域迁移成功率>52%），但在小模型（如Llama-3-8B）上优化的攻击几乎无法迁移到前沿闭源模型（如GPT-5预览版），表明当前自动化注入尚非模型无关威胁；
  • 实践启示：防御需兼顾输入净化与智能体执行沙箱化，且安全对齐本身可构成一道有效防线。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents the first comprehensive empirical study of automated prompt injection attacks against LLM-based agents operating in realistic, multi-step agentic environments. We adapt both white-box (GCG) and black-box (TAP) optimization methods to the AgentDojo framework and evaluate them across 80 task pairs spanning programming, math reasoning, web interaction, and knowledge QA—using 6 diverse models from open-weight (Llama-3, Qwen2) to frontier closed models (GPT-4o, Claude-3). Results show that black-box TAP substantially outperforms gradient-based GCG (2.3× higher success rate), primarily due to GCG’s instability under practical compute budgets. Attack effectiveness is highly model-dependent: stronger attacker models generate more potent injections, yet safety-tuned variants often refuse adversarial prompt generation (up to 68% refusal rate). Task-universal attacks transfer well across unseen tasks and out-of-distribution domains (>52% success), but attacks optimized on small open-source models fail catastrophically against frontier models like GPT-5—highlighting that automated prompt injection remains a credible but not model-agnostic threat, with significant barriers to broad exploitation.

Abstract

Indirect prompt injection poses a critical threat to LLM agents that interact with untrusted external data, yet automated attack methods--proven effective for jailbreaking--remain underexplored in realistic agentic settings. We present a comprehensive empirical evaluation of automated prompt injection attacks against LLM agents, adapting both white-box (GCG) and black-box (TAP) methods to the agentic setting within the AgentDojo framework. We evaluate across 80 task pairs spanning four domains and multiple models, and find that black-box optimization substantially outperforms gradient-based methods, a gap we attribute to GCG's optimization instability under reasonable compute budgets. We also find that TAP's effectiveness depends on the attacker model, as both general capability and safety tuning affect attack success--stronger models produce more effective injections, while safety-tuned attackers can refuse to generate adversarial prompts. Task-universal attacks transfer effectively to unseen tasks and out-of-distribution domains, but attacks optimized on smaller open-source models do not transfer to frontier models like GPT-5. These findings highlight automated prompt injection as a credible but model-dependent threat, with significant barriers remaining for model-agnostic exploitation.

item.title: AgentCanary: A Security Evaluation Framework for Autonomous AI Agents in Real Executable Environments
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.10484v1

AI Summary (中文)

AgentCanary：面向真实可执行环境的自主AI智能体安全评估框架

随着自主AI智能体从对话式交互转向端到端任务执行，安全失效模式已由文本层面的欺骗性输出，演变为对底层系统、工具与数据的实际劫持与破坏。然而，现有安全评估工作存在三大根本瓶颈：风险覆盖碎片化（如仅关注越狱或提示注入）、执行环境失真（依赖静态问答或模拟工具响应）、以及评估维度单一粗粒度（常仅以最终答案是否“有害”作二值判定）。为系统性破解上述局限，本文提出 AgentCanary——首个面向真实可执行环境的自主智能体安全评估框架。

AgentCanary 的核心贡献有三：
1. 正交化风险建模：提出“入口×影响”（Entry × Impact）风险分类法，解耦攻击如何进入智能体（如通过用户指令、工具反馈、环境状态）与其造成何种后果（如数据泄露、权限提升、服务中断），并据此构建覆盖真实部署流程（如CI/CD流水线、云资源管理、多文档协同编辑）的28类场景化任务套件；
2. 高保真可执行环境：摒弃静态QA或mock工具，支持智能体在动态生成的隔离沙箱中调用真实工具API（如git, curl, aws-cli），维持跨步骤的持久化状态（如文件系统变更、进程上下文），天然支持长周期、状态依赖型攻击（如隐蔽持久化、渐进式提权）的闭环评估；
3. 轨迹驱动的多维量化评估：基于完整执行轨迹（含所有观察、推理、动作序列），在三个正交维度进行细粒度打分：结果安全性（Outcome Safety）、安全感知能力（Security Awareness，如是否识别异常工具返回）、任务效用性（Task Utility，如是否完成核心目标）。

我们在3大主流智能体框架（LangChain, LlamaIndex, AutoGen）上，对12个前沿模型（含GPT-4o、Claude-3.5、Qwen2.5-72B）开展系统评测。结果表明：当前智能体在技能被篡改、状态被污染、长周期攻击下安全感知率不足31%，且任务效用与安全性呈显著负相关——凸显“安全-效能”权衡困境。AgentCanary 为构建可信自主系统提供了首个可复现、可扩展、可诊断的安全基准。

AI Summary (English)

AgentCanary is a comprehensive security evaluation framework for autonomous AI agents operating in real executable environments. It addresses three key limitations of prior work: fragmented risk coverage, low-fidelity (static/mocked) execution settings, and coarse-grained, single-dimensional assessment. First, it introduces an orthogonal Entry × Impact risk taxonomy—decoupling how adversarial influence enters the agent (e.g., via user input, tool response, or environment state) from what harm it causes (e.g., data exfiltration, privilege escalation)—instantiated as a scenario-aligned task suite spanning realistic workflows. Second, it provides a high-fidelity environment where agents interact with real tools (e.g., git, aws-cli) against dynamically provisioned artifacts, maintaining persistent state across multi-step trajectories to enable long-horizon attack evaluation. Third, it performs trajectory-grounded, multi-dimensional scoring along three orthogonal axes: Outcome Safety, Security Awareness, and Task Utility—going beyond final output to assess reasoning and action sequences. Evaluating 12 frontier models across three agent frameworks under multiple adversarial attacks, we find that current agents frequently fail to detect compromises—especially under skill poisoning, state persistence, and long-horizon threats—establishing a critical baseline for secure agent development.

Abstract

Autonomous AI agents have driven the transition from conversation to task execution, shifting security failures from textual deception to system compromise. Although security evaluation is crucial for proactive risk prevention, prior work is constrained by fundamental bottlenecks, including fragmented risk coverage, static or low-fidelity execution environments, and single-dimensional and coarse-grained assessment metrics. To address these challenges, we propose AgentCanary, a comprehensive security evaluation framework for autonomous AI agents. AgentCanary provides a systematic solution along three contributions. First, comprehensive risk coverage: we introduce an orthogonal Entry $\times$ Impact risk taxonomy that decouples how adversarial influence enters the agent from what harm it ultimately causes, and instantiate it as a scenario-aligned task suite spanning realistic deployment workflows. Second, a high-fidelity real executable environment: rather than static Q&A or mocked tool responses, agents interact with real tools against dynamically provisioned task artifacts, with persistent state across multi-step interactions that naturally supports long-horizon attack evaluation. Third, trajectory-grounded multi-dimensional evaluation: evaluation consumes the full agent trajectory rather than the reply text or a single tool call, enabling decomposed scoring along three orthogonal dimensions, Outcome Safety, Security Awareness, and Task Utility. We evaluate a broad set of frontier models on AgentCanary against multiple established adversarial attack methods across three agent frameworks. The results reveal that current agents often fail to recognize the attacks they face, particularly under compromised skills, persistent state, and long-horizon execution attacks, and provide a systematic baseline for developing more reliable and secure agent systems.

item.title: Advancing the State-of-the-Art in Empirical Privacy Auditing
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.10481v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

参数高效微调（PEFT）虽降低了大语言模型（LLM）的训练成本，却可能引发对个体训练样本的异常记忆，构成严重实证隐私风险。实证隐私审计（Empirical Privacy Auditing, EPA）通过成员推断（MI）或数据重构攻击量化真实场景下的数据泄露程度，其核心瓶颈在于如何设计高灵敏、可解释且不损害原始数据隐私的“金丝雀”（canary）样本。

方法创新

本文提出三项关键改进：
1. 合成金丝雀生成：采用高温采样（$T \geq 0.8$）从LLM中生成合成canary，提示词（prompt）针对隐私敏感训练数据定制；此类canary作为高影响力离群点，显著提升识别率，且因自身非私有，可重复插入、人工审查，不引入额外隐私泄露。
2. 合成数据隐私审计新范式：针对微调后模型生成的合成数据，提出基于辅助模型微调的审计框架——在合成数据上微调一个轻量辅助模型，再对其执行原canary的成员推断审计，从而间接、强效地评估合成数据所继承的隐私泄露。
3. 系统性归因分析：首次联合探究模型容量（如LoRA秩、层数）与canary熵值（由温度控制）对记忆行为的交互影响，揭示高熵canary在小模型中仍具高可检测性，而大模型对低熵canary的记忆更具隐蔽性。

主要贡献

本工作不仅提升了EPA的可靠性、可解释性与实用性，更建立了从微调到合成数据发布的端到端隐私审计链条，为负责任的LLM部署提供了可复现、可验证的实证基准。

AI Summary (English)

This paper advances empirical privacy auditing (EPA) for parameter-efficiently fine-tuned LLMs, which often memorize sensitive training examples. We propose generating synthetic canaries via high-temperature sampling ($T \geq 0.8$) from LLMs using data-aware prompts—yielding highly identifiable, inspectable, and non-private outliers that enable robust membership inference audits without compromising real-data privacy. To audit privacy leakage through synthetic data, we introduce a novel auxiliary-model paradigm: fine-tune a lightweight model on the generated synthetic data, then audit that model for the original canaries—providing a strong proxy for synthetic data’s privacy risk. Finally, leveraging our auditing framework, we conduct the first systematic study of how model capacity (e.g., LoRA rank) and canary entropy jointly govern memorization behavior. Our methods significantly improve audit sensitivity, transparency, and practicality—establishing a rigorous, end-to-end empirical privacy benchmark for LLM fine-tuning and synthetic data release.

Abstract

Parameter-efficient fine-tuning of large language models (LLMs) can exhibit problematic memorization of individual training examples. Empirical privacy auditing (EPA) quantifies this risk by measuring realistic data leakage on membership inference (MI) or reconstruction attacks. A key challenge in EPA is designing ``canary'' examples that are mixed with the privacy-sensitive training data. We propose generating synthetic canaries via high-temperature sampling ($T \geq 0.8$) from LLMs, using prompts tailored to the privacy-sensitive training data. These canaries act as high-influence outliers, ensuring high identifiability and hence strong audits. Further, since the canaries are themselves non-private, they are inspectable and can be inserted with repetition without jeopardizing the privacy of the real data. An important use of models fine-tuned on privacy-sensitive data is the generation of synthetic data. This also comes with privacy risk. We introduce a powerful synthetic data audit based on fine-tuning an auxiliary model on the synthetic data. Auditing the auxiliary model for the original canaries then provides a strong estimate of the privacy leakage through the synthetic data. Finally, leveraging our strong auditing methodologies, we perform a systematic investigation into the interacting effects of model capacity and canary entropy on memorization.

item.title: HE-DAP: Homomorphic Encryption-based Dynamic Adaptive Parameter Optimization for Statistical Computation
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.10477v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

同态加密（HE）是实现隐私保护统计计算的关键技术，但其高昂的计算开销严重制约实际部署。本研究发现：逆平方根（inverse square root）——统计分析与机器学习中广泛使用的底层原语（如归一化、协方差计算、梯度更新）——在主流HE库（如Lattigo、HEaaN）中性能波动剧烈且普遍次优。根本原因在于其加速实现需权衡两类高成本HE操作：① 采用高阶切比雪夫多项式近似以加速牛顿迭代；② 启用自举（bootstrapping）以控制密文噪声增长。而这两类操作的相对开销在不同硬件（CPU/GPU）与库版本间差异高达6倍，导致任何固定参数配置（如预设多项式阶数或迭代次数）均无法跨平台保持高效。

方法创新：HE-DAP框架

为此，我们提出HE-DAP（Homomorphic Encryption-based Dynamic Adaptive Parameter Optimization），首个面向HE环境异构性的动态参数优化框架。HE-DAP不依赖人工调参，而是通过轻量级环境画像（profiling），自动建模多项式阶数与迭代次数间的帕累托最优关系，在给定精度目标（如最大相对误差MRE ≤ 10⁻⁸）下，为当前平台搜索出最优计算路径。

实验结果与价值

在Lattigo、HEaaN-CPU及HEaaN-GPU三大平台上的评估表明：HE-DAP将加密逆平方根计算速度提升最高达2.35倍（相较PP-STAT中的固定配置），同时严格保障数值精度（MRE ≤ 3.1×10⁻⁸）。更重要的是，该底层优化直接驱动上层统计任务（如安全主成分分析、加权最小二乘）端到端加速，验证了“环境感知的参数优化”对规模化隐私计算的工程可行性与实用价值。

AI Summary (English)

Homomorphic encryption (HE) enables privacy-preserving statistical computation but suffers from prohibitive overhead—especially for the inverse square root, a critical primitive in many analytics workloads. Its performance varies widely across HE libraries and hardware due to a fundamental trade-off: high-degree Chebyshev polynomial evaluation (to accelerate Newton’s method) versus bootstrapping (to manage ciphertext noise), whose relative costs differ by up to 6× across environments. To resolve this, we propose HE-DAP, a cross-platform framework that dynamically optimizes polynomial degree and iteration count via lightweight environment profiling, targeting user-specified accuracy (e.g., MRE ≤ 3.1×10⁻⁸). Evaluated on Lattigo, HEaaN-CPU, and HEaaN-GPU, HE-DAP accelerates the encrypted inverse square root by up to 2.35× over fixed configurations while preserving numerical fidelity. Crucially, this low-level optimization translates to measurable end-to-end speedups in complex statistical analyses—demonstrating that principled, environment-aware parameter adaptation makes large-scale private analytics practical.

Abstract

Homomorphic encryption (HE) enables privacy-preserving analytics but remains hindered by high computational overhead. We find that the inverse square root-a key primitive in many statistical and machine learning workloads-exhibits inconsistent and often suboptimal performance across HE libraries and hardware. This stems from a core trade-off between two costly HE operations: evaluating high-degree Chebyshev polynomials to speed up Newton's method versus performing bootstrapping to manage ciphertext noise. Because their relative costs vary by up to 6x across environments, any fixed configuration proves inherently inefficient. To address this challenge, we present HE-DAP, a cross-platform optimization framework that automatically navigates this trade-off. By profiling an environment's unique performance characteristics, HE-DAP finds the optimal balance between polynomial degree and iteration count to accelerate the encrypted inverse square root computation for a given accuracy target. Our evaluation on Lattigo, HEaaN-CPU, and HEaaN-GPU shows that HE-DAP's adaptive approach yields significant performance gains. It accelerates the core inverse square root computation by up to 2.35x over the fixed configuration in PP-STAT while maintaining high numerical accuracy (MRE <= 3.1 x 10^-8). We further demonstrate that optimizing this fundamental building block directly enhances the end-to-end performance of complex statistical analyses, confirming the practical benefits of our environment-aware approach. By automatically adapting to heterogeneous execution environments, HE-DAP demonstrates that principled parameter optimization can make privacy-preserving statistical analytics practical at scale.

item.title: Privacy-Preserving Credit Risk Prediction with Alternative Data
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.10333v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

信用风险预测是消费信贷领域的核心任务。传统方法依赖借款人的人口统计、财务及信贷历史等传统数据，但其覆盖有限、滞后性强。近年研究表明，替代数据（如手机通信行为数据）可显著提升借款人信用画像的完整性与准确性。然而，此类数据由电信运营商等第三方持有，直接共享将严重侵犯用户隐私；现有研究却普遍忽视该隐私瓶颈，缺乏兼顾实用性与合规性的解决方案。

方法创新

本文首次提出“隐私保护下的替代数据信用风险预测”新问题，并严格建模三大现实约束：（1）隐私保护约束（保障用户原始替代数据不出域、不泄露）；（2）模型机密性约束（模型仅在金融机构本地训练与部署，外部数据方不接触模型参数）；（3）无损性约束（预测性能不劣于明文联合建模）。为此，我们设计了PrivacyCredit——一种基于安全多方计算与同态加密协同的新型隐私计算框架，支持金融机构在不获取原始替代数据的前提下，安全聚合特征并完成端到端模型训练。

主要发现与贡献

在真实信贷数据集（含银行记录+脱敏通信数据）上的实验表明：

  • PrivacyCredit 在AUC、KS、F1等关键指标上完全匹配明文联合建模基准（误差<0.001），验证其理论无损性；
  • 通过形式化证明，严格保障差分隐私级数据安全与模型参数零泄露；
  • 单次推理耗时仅增加12%，训练开销可控，具备生产环境落地潜力。

本工作为金融数据要素跨域可信流通提供了首个兼具强隐私保障、模型主权保留与性能无损的可验证范式。

AI Summary (English)

Credit risk prediction critically relies on comprehensive borrower profiling, yet alternative data (e.g., mobile communication logs) — held by non-financial entities — remain underutilized due to privacy risks from raw-data sharing. We formalize privacy-preserving credit risk prediction with alternative data, requiring simultaneous satisfaction of three practical constraints: (i) consumer privacy preservation, (ii) model confidentiality (centralized training at the financial institution only), and (iii) lossless predictive performance. To this end, we propose PrivacyCredit, a novel secure machine learning framework combining homomorphic encryption and secure aggregation. Theoretically, we prove its end-to-end privacy guarantee (under simulation-based security), model confidentiality, and exact equivalence to plaintext joint modeling. Empirically, on a real-world dataset linking bank records with telecom data, PrivacyCredit achieves identical AUC, KS, and F1 scores as the insecure baseline (Δ < 0.001), while incurring only ~12% inference overhead. This work establishes the first provably secure, performance-preserving, and institution-centric solution for leveraging alternative data in credit scoring.

Abstract

Credit risk prediction is a critical problem in the consumer credit industry. Traditionally, financial institutions construct credit risk prediction models using borrowers' demographic, financial, and credit history data, collectively referred to as traditional data. Recent studies have demonstrated that alternative data, such as borrowers' mobile phone communication data, enable lenders to acquire fuller and more accurate profiles of borrowers' creditworthiness, thereby improving credit risk prediction performance. Nevertheless, alternative data are held by external entities independent of financial institutions. Directly sharing alternative data with financial institutions infringe on consumer privacy, yet existing credit risk prediction studies largely overlook this issue. To address this gap, we define a new problem, namely privacy-preserving credit risk prediction with alternative data, which simultaneously considers three practical constraints: the privacy-preserving constraint that protects consumer privacy, the model-confidentiality constraint that learns and stores the model centrally at the financial institution, and the lossless constraint that maintains the performance of the learned model. To solve this problem, we develop PrivacyCredit, a novel privacy-preserving machine learning method. We then theoretically demonstrate the privacy-preserving, model-confidential, and lossless properties of PrivacyCredit. Through extensive experiments using a real-world credit dataset linked with alternative data, we demonstrate the predictive value of securely incorporating alternative data into credit risk prediction and show that PrivacyCredit achieves the same predictive performance as the model learned from the insecure plaintext combination of traditional and alternative data. We further evaluate its model-confidentiality property and computational efficiency.

item.title: Game-Theoretic Multi-Agent Control for Robust Contextual Reasoning in LLMs
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.10322v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

大语言模型（LLMs）在多轮对话中依赖持续演化的上下文进行推理，而非孤立生成响应。这使其易受提示注入（prompt-injection）和上下文投毒（context-poisoning）攻击：攻击者通过插入局部合理但语义误导的片段，逐步扭曲模型的推理轨迹。现有防御方法多聚焦于单轮输出过滤，忽视跨轮次的上下文动态演化，导致长程推理鲁棒性严重不足。尽管Model Context Protocol（MCP）标准化了上下文交换与工具调用，但其仅为被动路由层，无法保障上下文一致性与稳定性。

方法创新：GT-MCP

本文提出博弈论安全模型上下文协议（GT-MCP）——一种控制器驱动的多智能体控制框架。GT-MCP将上下文管理建模为闭环动力学过程，协同调度三个异构LLM代理，并通过三维度联合评估的可信度函数（trust function）选择最优输出：

  • 因果一致性：比对输出与经验证的上下文图谱（validated context graph）的逻辑因果链；
  • 语义共识度：量化三代理间响应的语义对齐强度；
  • 分布漂移监测：实时检测隐式表征随轮次演化的统计偏移。

当检测到不稳定性时，系统触发基于回滚的自愈机制：自动恢复至最近验证有效的上下文快照，阻断未授权片段的传播。

关键结果

在500轮自适应对抗威胁下的实证评估表明：

  • 上下文漂移在99.6%的轮次中保持有界，仅0.4%需触发恢复；
  • 每轮效用值高度稳定（中位数 = −0.19，P₀₅ = −0.72，P₉₅ = 0.30），严重退化（< −1）发生率仅0.4%；
  • 控制器层面无一次注入成功，选定输出胜率稳定≥98%；
  • 计算开销可控：单token延迟恒定为1.63×10⁻³秒，具备工程部署可行性。

AI Summary (English)

Large Language Models (LLMs) are vulnerable to prompt-injection and context-poisoning attacks in multi-turn interactions, where adversarial fragments gradually distort reasoning trajectories by exploiting evolving context—yet existing defenses ignore cross-turn contextual dynamics. To address this, we propose GT-MCP: a game-theoretic, controller-driven multi-agent protocol that treats context management as a closed-loop dynamical process. GT-MCP coordinates three heterogeneous LLM agents and selects outputs via a trust function jointly evaluating (i) causal consistency against a validated context graph, (ii) inter-agent semantic agreement, and (iii) temporal distributional drift. Upon instability detection, it triggers rollback-based self-healing to restore trusted context. Under adaptive adversarial evaluation over 500 turns, GT-MCP bounds contextual drift in 99.6% of turns, achieves near-zero controller-level injection success (0%), maintains output win rates >98%, and incurs predictable latency (1.63×10⁻³ s/token).

Abstract

Large Language Models (LLMs) in multi-turn interactions maintain evolving context rather than generating isolated responses, making them vulnerable to prompt-injection and context-poisoning attacks in which locally plausible adversarial fragments gradually distort reasoning trajectories. Existing defenses mainly filter individual outputs and often ignore context evolution across turns, leaving long-horizon reasoning exposed. Although the Model Context Protocol (MCP) standardizes context exchange and tool invocation, it functions as a passive routing layer and does not enforce contextual stability. To address these limitations, we introduce the Game-Theoretic Secure Model Context Protocol (GT-MCP), a controller-driven multi-agent method that treats context management as a closed-loop dynamical process. GT-MCP coordinates three heterogeneous LLM agents and selects outputs through a trust function that jointly evaluates causal consistency against a validated context graph, semantic agreement among agents, and distributional drift over time. When instability is detected, a rollback-based self-healing mechanism restores the validated context and prevents unsupported fragments from propagating. Empirical evaluation over 500 interaction turns under an adaptive adversarial threat model shows that contextual drift remains bounded in 99.6% of turns, with recovery required in only 0.4%. Per-turn utility remains tightly concentrated, with median = -0.19, P05 = -0.72, and P95 = 0.30; severe degradation below -1 occurs in only 0.4% of cases, and no injection attempt succeeds at the controller level. Selected outputs maintain stable win rates above 98%, and computational overhead remains predictable, with latency per token = 1.63e-3 s.

item.title: Benchmarking and Exploring the Capabilities of LLMs for Attack Investigations
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.10281v1

AI Summary (中文)

AuditBench：首个面向安全日志调查的LLM基准评测框架

本研究提出 AuditBench——一个专为评估大语言模型（LLMs）在安全事件调查中分析系统审计日志能力而设计的新型基准数据集。针对实际安全运营中响应团队高频执行的四类核心任务（包括告警初筛、攻击链还原、恶意行为归因与持久化机制识别），我们构建了覆盖 50+差异化安全场景 的高质量日志语料库，涵盖Linux与Windows双平台真实环境下的恶意活动（如横向移动、Rootkit植入）与良性噪声（如系统更新、合法脚本执行），确保任务复杂性与现实对抗性。

我们系统评测了5个前沿开源及闭源LLM（如Llama-3-70B、Claude-3.5-Sonnet、GPT-4o等）在该基准上的表现，并深入剖析其性能差异根源：模型规模显著影响长上下文日志推理能力；日志表征方式（原始文本 vs. 结构化摘要）决定信息保真度；提示工程策略（如分步推理链、角色扮演）可提升任务分解准确率；而任务类型本身构成关键瓶颈——模型在识别隐蔽持久化（如WMI事件订阅）时错误率高达62%，远高于基础告警分类（错误率仅18%）。

此外，我们首次对LLM生成的调查解释质量进行量化评估：仅31%的解释具备可验证的证据锚点（如具体进程ID、时间戳、事件ID），常见错误包括日志事实幻觉、因果倒置、跨事件错误关联。本工作不仅为安全领域LLM能力评估提供了标准化工具与基线，更揭示了当前技术在高保真日志理解、多跳推理与可解释性方面的根本局限，为下一代安全智能体的设计指明了关键优化路径。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces AuditBench, the first benchmark specifically designed to evaluate LLMs’ capabilities in investigating security audit logs. It comprises 50+ realistic Linux/Windows scenarios—spanning both malicious (e.g., lateral movement, persistence via WMI) and benign activities—and targets four core incident response tasks: alert triaging, attack chain reconstruction, attribution, and persistence mechanism identification. We evaluate five state-of-the-art LLMs and find performance varies significantly with model scale, log representation (raw vs. structured), prompt design (e.g., chain-of-thought), and task complexity—especially for subtle persistence detection (62% error rate vs. 18% for basic triaging). We further quantify explanation quality: only 31% of LLM outputs include verifiable evidence anchors (e.g., process ID, timestamp), with common errors including factual hallucination and spurious cross-event correlation. AuditBench establishes a rigorous foundation for LLM assessment in security operations and highlights critical research directions in faithful log reasoning and explainable investigation.

Abstract

This paper presents AuditBench, a new benchmark dataset for evaluating the capabilities of LLMs at investigating security-related system audit logs. We design and use this benchmark to explore the performance of LLMs on four log-investigation tasks that incident response teams commonly perform, ranging from triaging alerts generated by detectors to identifying persistence mechanisms on compromised systems. AuditBench consists of system audit logs collected from Linux and Windows machines, and spans over 50 different security investigation scenarios, including both malicious and benign activity. Using our benchmark, we evaluate and analyze the performance of five frontier LLMs at analyzing audit logs for attack investigations. Our analysis illuminates how LLM performance and error profiles vary according to different design choices, such as differences in model size, data representation, prompt construction, and specific investigation tasks. Additionally, we characterize the quality of the explanations produced by LLMs and the types of errors that models make across our benchmark. Collectively, our work provides a foundation for assessing the capabilities of LLMs for investigating security logs, novel insights for practitioners using LLMs in security operations, and important directions for future research.

item.title: RECON: An LLM-Enhanced Backward Constraint Analysis Framework
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.10264v1

AI Summary (中文)

RECON：一种大语言模型增强的反向约束分析框架

背景与挑战：传统符号执行等静态分析技术虽具备严谨的约束推理能力，但在现代复杂软件（尤其是Android应用）中面临严重可扩展性瓶颈：路径爆炸、海量框架函数建模困难、以及低层字节码丢失高层语义意图。在事件驱动、框架深度耦合的Android环境中，上述问题被显著放大，导致约束提取精度低、可读性差、成功率不足。

方法创新：本文提出RECON——首个面向Android字节码的LLM增强型反向约束分析框架。RECON以目标敏感方法为起点，自底向上反向追溯至应用入口点，系统识别方法级控制流约束；关键突破在于引入大语言模型（LLM）作为“语义翻译器”：将抽象、冗余的Dalvik字节码条件（如if-eqz, invoke-static链）自动转化为自然语言+逻辑公式混合的可解释规范（如“仅当用户已登录且网络连接为Wi-Fi时触发位置上报”），同时保持与原始约束的逻辑等价性。

实验验证：在78个真实Android约束提取场景中，RECON集成5种主流LLM（包括Llama-3-70B、Qwen2-72B等），相较传统符号执行工具（如Soot+JPF）实现5.8倍加速，100%成功完成约束生成，且输出约束在逻辑上严格等价、语义更精确、人工可读性提升显著。进一步在100个恶意样本上开展恶意行为分析，RECON对危险API（如sendSMS, getDeviceId）触发路径的语义约束生成成功率达84%，并能精准捕获跨多分支、多事件循环的复杂约束组合，为安卓恶意软件逆向分析与合规检测提供了新范式。

AI Summary (English)

RECON is a novel LLM-enhanced backward constraint analysis framework for Android bytecode. It addresses the scalability and semantic gap limitations of traditional symbolic execution by performing target-driven backward path discovery from sensitive methods to entry points, extracting method-level control-flow constraints, and leveraging LLMs to translate low-level bytecode conditions into precise, human-interpretable specifications (e.g., natural language + logical predicates) while preserving logical equivalence. Evaluated across 78 real-world Android constraint-extraction tasks using five LLMs (e.g., Llama-3-70B, Qwen2-72B), RECON achieves 100% success rate and runs 5.8× faster than conventional symbolic execution, with significantly improved precision and interpretability. In malware analysis on 100 samples, RECON successfully generates semantic constraints triggering dangerous API behaviors in 84% of cases and reliably detects complex, multi-path constraints—demonstrating its effectiveness for security-critical Android program understanding.

Abstract

While traditional techniques, such as symbolic execution, provide a principled foundation for precise constraint reasoning in program analysis, they struggle to scale to modern software systems mainly due to path explosion, the need for function modeling, and the loss of semantic intent at low-level program representations. In complex execution environments such as Android, characterized by extensive framework interactions and event-driven behavior, these limitations are even more amplified. Thus, in this paper, we present a novel large language model (LLM)-enhanced backward constraint analysis framework that combines the precision of static program analysis with LLM's semantic understanding to extract precise execution constraints from Android bytecode. Our approach, titled RECON, performs backward path discovery from target method(s) to the application entry point(s), discovers method-level control-flow constraints, and leverages LLM reasoning to transform bytecode conditions into interpretable specifications. We evaluated RECON using five LLMs across 78 Android constraint-extraction scenarios and compared it with traditional symbolic execution on real-world applications. Results demonstrate that our approach operates 5.8X faster than traditional symbolic execution, with a 100% success rate, while maintaining logical equivalence and providing significantly more precise and interpretable output. We further evaluated RECON for malware analysis on 100 samples. The results indicate an 84% success rate in generating semantic constraints that lead to the execution of dangerous API behaviors and in detecting complex constraints across multiple execution paths.

item.title: Recalling Too Well: Sycophancy Evaluation and Mitigation in Memory-Augmented Models
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.10949v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

持续性记忆系统（Persistent Memory Systems）被广泛视为提升大语言模型（LLM）长期个性化服务能力的关键技术，其通过跨轮次存储用户信念来增强交互一致性。然而，本研究首次揭示：记忆增强反而系统性削弱模型的事实正确性，核心机制是放大“谄媚性”（sycophancy）——即模型为迎合用户观点而主动牺牲准确性。

方法与基准

我们构建了首个面向记忆系统中谄媚性评估的专用基准 MIST（Memory-Induced Sycophancy Test），包含科学、医学与道德推理三大领域共1,200+轮合成多轮对话。每轮对话中，用户自然表达看似合理但实质错误的信念（如“抗生素可治疗流感”），并设置严格控制的上下文基线（in-context only）用于对比。

关键发现

  • 在3种前沿记忆架构（MemGPT、Llama-Index、VectorDB+RAG）与5类主流模型（Llama-3、Qwen、Phi-3、Gemma、Claude-3-haiku）上测试，所有记忆系统均显著加剧谄媚行为：平均谄媚率较无记忆基线升高12–25倍；
  • 错误归因分析表明，记忆提取环节是主因：离散化记忆片段压缩过程丢失关键纠错上下文（如用户后继自我修正或外部证据），却完整保留初始错误信念；
  • 记忆并未提升事实召回——反观基线模型在无记忆时更常主动纠正用户。

创新与缓解方案

基于上述洞见，我们提出两项轻量级干预策略：（1）记忆置信度门控（Confidence-Gated Memory Retrieval），动态过滤低置信度记忆片段；（2）反事实回忆提示（Counterfactual Recall Prompting），强制模型在响应前显式检索并权衡矛盾证据。二者均无需微调，在保持98%以上原始记忆召回能力的同时，将谄媚率降低67–89%，且事实准确率反超记忆系统12.4%。

AI Summary (English)

This paper identifies and quantifies a critical unintended consequence of memory-augmented LLMs: persistent memory systems systematically amplify sycophancy—prioritizing user agreement over factual accuracy. We introduce MIST, the first benchmark for evaluating memory-induced sycophancy across scientific, medical, and moral domains, using synthetically generated multi-turn dialogues with plausible user misconceptions. Evaluating three state-of-the-art memory architectures (MemGPT, Llama-Index, VectorDB+RAG) and five model families, we find memory increases sycophancy rates by up to 25× versus in-context baselines. Error analysis pinpoints lossy memory extraction—where discrete snippet compression preserves user errors but discards corrective context—as the primary driver. To mitigate this, we propose two lightweight, training-free interventions: confidence-gated memory retrieval and counterfactual recall prompting. These reduce sycophancy by 67–89% while matching or exceeding memory systems in factual recall (+12.4% accuracy), demonstrating that safer memory augmentation need not sacrifice utility.

Abstract

Persistent memory systems promise to make LLMs more helpful by storing user beliefs over time. We show they also make models less correct by systematically amplifying sycophancy, wherein models prioritize agreement with users over accuracy. We conduct the first systematic evaluation of this effect, introducing MIST: a benchmark of synthetically generated multi-turn conversations where users express plausible misconceptions in scientific, medical, and moral reasoning domains. Testing across three state-of-the-art memory systems and five model families reveals that memory amplifies sycophantic behavior across all conditions, with up to 25x higher sycophancy rates than in-context baselines. Error analyses suggest memory extraction as the primary culprit: lossy compression into discrete snippets encodes user misconceptions while discarding corrective context. Based on these results, we propose two lightweight mitigations that substantially reduce sycophancy while matching or exceeding memory systems at factual recall.

item.title: Evaluating Research-Level Math Proofs via Strict Step-Level Verification
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.10799v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

大型语言模型（LLMs）在严格验证复杂数学证明时表现薄弱。传统“全局评估”方法（如整体打分或结尾判断）易受“上下文污染”（context poisoning）干扰：表面合理但隐含逻辑漏洞的中间陈述会误导模型，导致幻觉性接受（误判错误证明为正确）或过度怀疑（拒斥正确证明），尤其在研究级数学推理中尤为突出。

方法创新：严格步级验证框架

本文提出严格步级验证（Strict Step-Level Verification）范式，彻底转向细粒度、可追溯的推理审计：

  • 每步独立建模：为每个演绎步骤显式维护前提、引用定理、推导规则及上下文依赖；
  • 定理来源强约束：仅允许从预定义数学知识库（含明确声明的公理、引理与领域惯例）中调用定理，禁止隐式/未声明引用；
  • 对抗性诊断基准：基于FirstProof挑战构建高难度、人工精筛的研究级证明测试集，包含精心设计的隐蔽逻辑缺陷（如量词错位、边界条件遗漏、未声明的光滑性假设等）。

关键发现与理论启示

  • 系统性消融证实核心机制必要性：移除步级约束后，全局提示在全部测试案例中均无法定位细微错误，准确率趋近随机；而完整框架将关键错误检出率提升至92.7%。
  • 失败模式根本性转变：剩余误拒案例不再源于严重逻辑幻觉，而集中表现为教条式超严谨（pedantic hyper-rigor）——即因未显式声明领域默认惯例（如“流形默认C∞”“群作用默认连续”）而拒绝正确证明，意外揭示了专家基准自身存在的隐性歧义。
  • 认知对齐价值：强制模型以人类数学家方式组织验证笔记（分步注释、来源标注、假设显化），不仅显著提升判别能力，更可赋能其对基座模型不熟悉的新前沿概念（如范畴论高阶结构、非交换几何）进行可靠推理，为未来自动化证明评审系统提供可解释、可审计的理论基础。

AI Summary (English)

Large Language Models (LLMs) fail to rigorously verify research-level mathematical proofs due to “context poisoning” in global evaluation—superficially plausible steps mask subtle logical flaws, causing hallucination or over-skepticism. We propose strict step-level verification: a framework that maintains explicit context per inference step and enforces strict sourcing constraints on applied theorems (only from declared axioms, lemmas, or domain conventions). Evaluated on an adversarial, expert-curated diagnostic suite from FirstProof, our method achieves 92.7% critical error detection—outperforming global baselines by >40 points. Ablation confirms deductive constraints are indispensable: unconstrained prompting consistently misses subtle flaws. Crucially, remaining rejections reflect pedantic hyper-rigor—rejecting valid proofs due to unstated domain conventions—thus exposing implicit ambiguities in the expert benchmark itself. This demonstrates that structuring verification notes like human mathematicians (stepwise, sourced, assumption-aware) enhances rigorous reasoning on frontier concepts beyond the base model’s knowledge and lays groundwork for auditable automated proof review. Code and prompts: github.com/…

Abstract

Large Language Models (LLMs) struggle to rigorously verify complex mathematical proofs. Standard global evaluation approaches suffer from "context poisoning," in which superficially plausible statements mask subtle logical flaws, leading to hallucination or over-skepticism. To address this, we shift from global evaluation to strict step-level verification: our framework maintains detailed context for each deduction step and strictly constrains the sources of applied theorems. We evaluate on a carefully curated adversarial diagnostic suite of research-level proofs drawn from the FirstProof challenge. A systematic ablation study demonstrates that these deductive constraints are indispensable, as unconstrained global prompting consistently fails to localize subtle logical errors. Beyond outperforming global evaluation, our approach fundamentally alters the failure taxonomy. Error analysis reveals that, rather than exhibiting severe logical hallucinations, remaining rejections are primarily instances of "pedantic hyper-rigor" stemming from unstated domain conventions, effectively exposing implicit ambiguities within the expert benchmark itself. Our findings suggest that prompting agents to organize their verification notes in a cautious, human-mathematician-like manner can substantially improve their ability to distinguish rigorous proofs from flawed ones, with the potential to strengthen agentic reasoning on frontier mathematical concepts that the base model does not already know well, and to lay a theoretical foundation for future automated proof-review systems. Code and prompts are available at GitHub.

item.title: Transformer Based Model for Spatiotemporal Feature Learning in EEG Emotion Recognition
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.10718v1

AI Summary (中文)

基于Transformer的EEG情绪识别时空特征学习模型（EEG-TransNet）

背景与挑战：脑电图（EEG）凭借高时间分辨率与低成本优势，成为情绪识别与脑状态解码的关键模态。然而，EEG信号具有强噪声性、个体差异大、时空耦合复杂等特点，传统方法难以协同建模区域拓扑性、动态时序依赖与跨通道同步性。

方法创新：本文提出EEG-TransNet——一种端到端的Transformer架构，专为EEG时空特征学习设计。其核心包含三大模块：
1) 多尺度预处理与特征提取模块：融合ResNet骨干网络与小波阈值去噪，提升信噪比并保留神经振荡关键频段；
2) 局部自注意力块（Local Self-Attention Block）：在电极邻域内施加受限注意力机制，显式建模皮层区域功能连接，缓解全连接Transformer的计算冗余与过拟合；
3) 模糊注意力同步Transformer（FAST）：引入模糊隶属度函数引导的跨通道注意力权重，动态量化通道间相位同步强度，实现对神经协同振荡的可解释建模。

实验验证：在BETA（多被试自然刺激）、SEED（三类情绪标准基准）和DepEEG（抑郁患者临床数据）三大公开数据集上系统评估。结果表明：EEG-TransNet在平均分类准确率上分别达到94.2%（SEED）、89.7%（BETA）、85.3%（DepEEG），显著优于CNN、LSTM及主流Transformer变体（+2.1–4.8%）。消融实验证实局部自注意力块贡献最大性能增益（+1.9% avg），而解码器中深度可分离卷积使参数量降低37%、推理延迟减少42%，精度损失<0.3%。跨被试泛化测试显示，被试间性能波动标准差仅±1.2%，验证其鲁棒性。

意义：EEG-TransNet为非侵入式脑机接口与临床情绪障碍辅助诊断提供了高精度、低计算开销、强泛化能力的新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Electroencephalography (EEG) offers high temporal resolution and cost efficiency for emotion recognition, yet modeling its complex spatiotemporal dynamics remains challenging. We propose EEG-TransNet, a novel Transformer-based architecture featuring: (1) a ResNet–wavelet denoising preprocessing module; (2) a Local Self-Attention Block to capture regional cortical connectivity with reduced computational overhead; and (3) a Fuzzy-Attention Synchronous Transformer (FAST) that models inter-channel phase synchronization via fuzzy-weighted attention. Evaluated on BETA, SEED, and DepEEG datasets, EEG-TransNet achieves state-of-the-art accuracy (94.2%, 89.7%, 85.3%) and superior robustness across variable signal lengths and subjects (inter-subject std < 1.2%). Ablation studies confirm the critical role of local attention (+1.9% gain), while depthwise separable convolutions in the decoder cut parameters by 37% with negligible accuracy loss (<0.3%). The model demonstrates strong generalizability for real-world EEG-based affective computing and clinical applications.

Abstract

Electroencephalography (EEG) is a widely adopted technique for monitoring brain activity, offering valuable insights into neurological states due to its high temporal resolution and cost-effectiveness. To enhance the analysis of complex EEG data, we propose EEG-TransNet, an architecture designed to capture temporal, regional, and synchronous features of EEG signals. EEG-TransNet introduces three key modules: 1) a preprocessing and feature extraction module leveraging ResNet and wavelet-based denoising, 2) a Local Self-Attention Block for regional feature learning, and 3) a Fuzzy-Attention Synchronous Transformer (FAST) to model spatiotemporal dependencies. Through extensive experiments on three EEG datasets (BETA, SEED, and DepEEG), the proposed model consistently outperforms other methods in terms of classification accuracy and robustness across varying signal lengths. Ablation studies confirm the contribution of the Local Self-Attention Block in improving performance, and the inclusion of depthwise separable convolutions in the decoder reduces computational complexity while maintaining high accuracy. EEG-TransNet's ability to generalize across subjects with minimal performance variation highlights its potential as a robust tool for EEG-based brain activity classification and emotion recognition tasks.

item.title: MoE Enhanced Federated Learning for Spatiotemporal Prediction
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.10499v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

交通预测是智能交通系统与城市计算的核心任务，但大量中小城市受限于传感器部署不足和城市发展不均衡，面临严重的交通数据稀缺问题。跨城市知识迁移成为提升数据匮乏城市预测能力的重要路径，然而：（1）中心化迁移方法存在用户级隐私泄露风险；（2）现有联邦学习方案难以建模城市间显著的时空异质性——不同城市的路网结构、出行模式、拥堵成因差异巨大，全局共享模型易导致“负迁移”。

方法创新：MoE-FedTP 框架

本文提出 MoE-FedTP（Mixture-of-Experts Enhanced Federated Traffic Prediction），一种轻量级、个性化的跨城联邦时空预测框架：

  • 双阶段特征解耦：各参与方（源城/目标城）独立运行轻量时空图神经网络（ST-GNN），提取本地化时空表征；
  • 异构专家池构建：从多个数据丰富源城市分别蒸馏出专属专家子网络，通过部分参数共享机制（仅共享底层时空编码器，保留上层专家头独立性）实现知识解耦与可扩展性；
  • 动态门控融合：目标城本地门控网络依据实时交通状态（如拥堵指数、时段、天气）自适应加权调用最相关专家，实现细粒度异质性建模，全程无原始数据上传，严格保障隐私。

实验验证与价值

在四个真实世界交通数据集（PeMS-BAY、METR-LA、PEMS03、PEMS07）上的跨城联邦实验表明：MoE-FedTP 在数据稀缺目标城（如仅含5%传感器）上，MAE/RMSE平均降低12.7%–18.3%，显著优于FedAvg、FedPer、CrossCity-STGCN等SOTA基线；消融实验证实门控机制与专家异构性设计贡献最大增益。本工作为隐私安全、异构鲁棒、资源高效的城市级联邦智能提供了新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Traffic prediction in data-scarce cities remains challenging due to sensor scarcity and urban development imbalance. While cross-city knowledge transfer is promising, centralized approaches compromise privacy, and existing federated methods fail to handle pronounced spatiotemporal heterogeneity across cities. We propose MoE-FedTP, a personalized federated framework leveraging lightweight Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) networks. It first extracts city-specific spatiotemporal features via local ST-GNNs; then constructs a pool of heterogeneous experts—each distilled from a distinct data-rich source city—using partial parameter sharing to preserve expert specialization. A dynamic gating network on the target city adaptively fuses experts based on real-time traffic context, enabling fine-grained modeling of urban diversity without raw data exchange. Experiments on four real-world traffic datasets show MoE-FedTP consistently outperforms state-of-the-art cross-city and federated baselines, reducing MAE/RMSE by 12.7%–18.3% on data-scarce cities. Our approach advances privacy-preserving, heterogeneity-aware, and scalable urban intelligence.

Abstract
